reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome (no-GPU) end-to-end across all 5 pages, desktop+mobile+light/dark screenshots read

rubric.evidence: Source calls a REAL WebLLM path: worker.js imports createEngine + streamChat from lib/webllm.js and streams via engine.chat.completions.create({stream:true}); the page gates on webGPUAdapterAvailable() before load. Verified the MLC id gemma3-1b-it-q4f16_1-MLC EXISTS in @mlc-ai/web-llm 0.2.84 prebuiltAppConfig (esm.run serves 0.2.84) — vram_required_MB 711, context 4096. In the no-GPU headless runner every page reached the honest 'unsupported / needs-WebGPU' state — NEVER a faked token.
rubric.notes: Real streamed token output on a GPU device was not observable in this no-GPU sandbox by design (WebLLM is WebGPU-only, no WASM fallback). The load path + MLC id are verified; a GPU-device run to observe live decode is the open item. Note the MLC id is gemma3-1b-it (no dash), distinct from the gemma-2-2b-it build.

rubric.evidence: Expected shape is a streamed chat continuation conditioned on the (system-folded) Gemma turn template. worker.js streams answer deltas via the shared streamChat; the UI appends token-by-token into the bubble; see-inside shows ttft/decode-time/tok/s/generated/context. Practical page runs JSON.parse() on the output to prove machine-readable shape; multi-model page shows MiniLM cosine scores + Gemma's grounded answer; the wire panel shows the exact system-folded messages array sent to the model.
rubric.notes: Semantic sanity is verified in code + the wire/see-inside surfaces; observing exact streamed tokens requires a GPU device (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (google/gemma-3-1b-it linked to card), declares-task (text-generation), declares-backend (WebGPU), declares-quantisation (q4f16_1), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance (read in the desktop light screenshot) lists model, MLC build gemma3-1b-it-q4f16_1-MLC, runtime WebLLM/MLC (not Transformers.js), params 1B q4f16_1, Gemma turn template with no system role (folded), backend WebGPU-only, download ~0.9 GB, context 4096, license Gemma Terms.
rubric.notes: Honest, complete disclosure; matches what worker.js loads (MODEL_ID gemma3-1b-it-q4f16_1-MLC via createEngine).

rubric.evidence: Every page uses the shared createModelLoader (no bespoke Load button). WebGPU gate fires first: on the no-GPU runner the loader lands in 'unsupported' with the honest needs-WebGPU message and NO action button, and the fallback panel explains how to enable WebGPU + the ~0.9 GB cost — no silent download. Per-model 'Clear cached model' control is provided by the shared loader once ready. On the multi-model page the MiniLM (Transformers.js, no-GPU) loader correctly shows the absent→'Download model (~25 MB)' state on a fresh profile.
rubric.notes: On a GPU device the absent→Download / current→auto-init(no button) / partial→Re-download matrix is driven by the same shared loader as the other WebLLM pages; the needs-WebGPU honest gate + honest absent MiniLM state are what this environment proves.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live on every page), errors-on-the-page (.err surface + Retry via loader), webgpu-capability-honest (real adapter probe → labelled unsupported). Driven runs: 0 uncaught JS errors, 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests on all 5 pages at desktop AND mobile. Stop calls engine.interruptGenerate(); generation failures set status .err.
rubric.notes: Honest needs-WebGPU rendered on all pages. Offline-cached / partial-evicted drives need a GPU device with a warm cache (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: Main: system-prompt textarea, temp/top_p/max-tokens ranges with live value bind, message textarea, Send/Stop/Clear. Basics: example chips + Ask. Practical: mode <select> (reasoning vs JSON) + example chips + schema input + Run, with live JSON.parse validity. Wild: persona chips + persona textarea + chat + New-scene. Multi-model: two shared loaders + KB textarea + question. Every control is a real labelled element; chips ≥ ~2.2rem tap target.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced via rendered screenshots; a full keyboard-only traversal on a GPU device is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run every output area shows a muted placeholder; on the no-GPU runner the loader shows the honest unsupported state and NO tokens are ever synthesised. The reply only ever comes from engine.chat.completions.create — no canned path.
rubric.notes: Verified across all 5 pages: no pre-baked result; the WebGPU gate prevents any output when the model can't run.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (no content ), buttons-have-names (all buttons have text). role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; chat log + answer aria-live=polite; textareas have associated labels; the sysbox/persona textareas are label-associated; caret/blink animation disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the streaming transcript on a GPU device would further validate.

rubric.evidence: Driven matrix: documentElement.scrollWidth - clientWidth == 0 (no horizontal overflow) at 1280x800 AND 360x740 (DPR3, touch) on all 5 pages. Screenshots read: gemma3 main (desktop light) — warm paper theme, at-a-glance table wraps cleanly, lede legible; mobile main reflows with no overflow. min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere on bubbles/flex items; grids use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr).
rubric.notes: Support recorded needs-review→ok pending the responsive-check seed; driven no-overflow pass across desktop+mobile is strong evidence.

rubric.evidence: All inference runs in a dedicated module Web Worker (worker.js, new Worker(url,{type:'module'})) so decode never blocks the main thread; tokens post incrementally via the shared streamChat callback and the UI only appends text. conformance worker-compliance=source-pass, inference-off-main-thread=pass. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture). Being the lightest WebLLM model (~0.9 GB, vram 711 MB) it is the most mobile-feasible chat page here.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement on a GPU device would confirm; the off-thread worker + incremental streaming is the key mitigation, matching the reference WebLLM pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream LLM tokens incrementally without blocking main thread INP web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread and stream incrementally so it never becomes a long task that spikes INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation runs in models/gemma-3-webllm/worker.js via the shared streamChat; the worker posts small deltas the main thread just appends. No synchronous main-thread token loop.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance worker-compliance + inference-off-main-thread pass; driven runs stayed responsive with 0 console errors on all 5 pages

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel form controls no horizontal overflow narrow mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + gap + intrinsic sizing and min-inline-size:0 on flex items with long content; grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr) so columns stack.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — composer/controls/chips use flex-wrap+gap; chat bubbles + composer textarea set min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere; multi-model loader/result grids use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,Npx),1fr); glance cells overflow-wrap:anywhere.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: scrollWidth-clientWidth==0 at 360x740 and 1280x800 on all 5 pages; gemma3-main light screenshot shows the glance table wrapping with no overflow

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live labelled controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region, label every control, keep buttons named + keyboard-operable, respect prefers-reduced-motion.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status; aria-live=polite chat log + answer; aria-label/associated labels on textareas; example/persona chips grouped; caret animation disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region=pass, buttons-have-names=pass; DOM inspection + rendered screenshots

guidanceConsulted.query: textarea that grows to fit content field-sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use field-sizing:content with min/max block-size so composer/system textareas grow with input.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (shared CHAT_CSS) — .composer textarea and .sysbox textarea use field-sizing:content with block-size caps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: CHAT_CSS in models/gemma-3-webllm/webllm-chat.js; no overflow observed on mobile

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); every page links the shared stylesheet and crumbs back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs present on all 5 pages (read in screenshots)

openQuestions: On a real WebGPU device: observe live streamed decode + measure ttft/tok/s in the see-inside panel, and confirm the system-folded Gemma turn template produces coherent replies.
Keyboard-only traversal + visible focus across all 5 pages on a GPU device (Enter-to-send/Shift+Enter, the practical mode <select>).
Drive the warm-cache auto-init (no button) + partial/evicted + offline-cached states on a GPU device with the ~0.9 GB model cached.
On a constrained mobile GPU, confirm the ~0.9 GB / 711 MB-vram build actually loads within budget — this is the lightest WebLLM page so it is the best mobile candidate to validate.

followUpGoals.goal: GPU-device verification pass for gemma-3-webllm: capture live streamed output + ttft/tok/s as retained evidence, on both desktop and a real mobile GPU (it is the lightest WebLLM model, the best mobile candidate).
Add a Gemma 3 long-context demo that uses more of its native context window than the 4096-token MLC build cap allows, if/when a larger-context MLC build ships.
Add a mobile memory-pressure pre-check before the ~0.9 GB download that links even-smaller fallbacks when a device can't fit it.

summary: gemma-3-webllm is an honest, genuinely-distinct demo: the catalogue's NEWEST and LIGHTEST WebLLM chat model, running gemma3-1b-it-q4f16_1-MLC (verified present in @mlc-ai/web-llm 0.2.84 prebuiltAppConfig; note the no-dash gemma3 id, distinct from the gemma-2-2b build) on WebLLM/WebGPU at ~0.9 GB / 711 MB vram — the most mobile-feasible chat page here. Full ladder: basics chat, practical (reasoning + strict JSON with live JSON.parse validation), wild persona role-play, and multi-model RAG (MiniLM retriever + Gemma generator). Driven end-to-end headless (no-GPU): honest needs-WebGPU on every WebLLM page + honest absent MiniLM state on multi-model (no faked tokens, no silent download), 0 JS/console errors, 0 failed requests, and no horizontal overflow at 1280x800 or 360x740 across all 5 pages, light + dark both legible. Real streamed decode verified by source + MLC-id existence; observing live tokens needs a GPU device (the high-priority follow-up). Revision 1.
